Chapter 6: Stirring Shadows

Kodipur ki raatein restless ho gayi thi. Chahe Mahadevi ka shraap toot chuka tha, lekin gaon mein ab ek alag tension thi — subtle lekin unmistakable. Windows bina wajah se creak karti, faint whispers hawa ke saath travel karti, aur bamboo grove har field ke kinare se silently dekhta raha.

Raghav ne ise pehle apne sapnon mein mehsoos kiya. Shadows moonlight ke against move kar rahe the, uska naam pukar rahe the — fragmented aur urgent. Phir gaon waalon ne chhote disturbances notice kiye — tools misplaced, lanterns invisible gusts se bujh jaana, empty streets par footsteps ki echo.

Ek shaam, Raghav bargad ke paas se guzar raha tha, blackened wooden box lekar, jab usne ek young boy ko roots ke paas khada dekha. Bachcha gaon mein naya tha, harvest festival ke liye relatives ke paas aaya hua. Woh tree ki taraf haath badha raha tha, nervously haste hue.

“Use mat chhoo,” Raghav ne tez awaaz mein kaha. Bachcha ruk gaya, wide eyes se use dekh raha tha.

“Kyun nahi? Yeh toh sirf ek ped hai,” bachche ne kaha.

Raghav ka seena tight ho gaya. Use roots ke saath faint ripples dikhi — subtle lekin menacing, jaise ped ne khud bachche ke approach ko mehsoos kar liya ho. Phir phusphusahat shuru hui — pehle halki, phir urgency se bhari: “Bloodline… caution… respect… heed or suffer…”

Pehle woh move kar pata, ek gust of wind ne bachche ka scarf utha diya — uske arms par glowing marks dikhe — land se judhi spirits ke distress ke symbols. Bachche ki hasi ruki, whimper mein badal gayi jab hawa unke aas-paas thick hone lagi.

Raghav tezi se move kiya, bachche aur ped ke beech khud ko place kiya. “Peeche hat!” usne cheekha. Zameen thodi si hili, aur roots se nikli floating lights violently swirl karne lagi, bachche ke energy ki taraf attract hote hue.

Dadi Shanta square ke kinare appear hui, chehra pale. “Woh sensitive hai,” unhone urgently kaha. “Kuch bachche woh mehsoos kar sakte hain jo adults nahi kar sakte. Uski presence ne Mahadevi ke release ke baad bache fragments ko stir kiya hai. Woh restless, curious… aur dangerous agar provoke kiye gaye.”

Bachcha ne Raghav ka haath pakad liya, kaanp raha tha. “Maine kuch galat nahi kiya… main bas ped dekhna chahta tha.”

Raghav uske paas baith gaya. “Theek hai. Ped ab shaant hai, lekin gaon mein sab kuch chain mein nahi hai. Spirits hain… fragments… unhe acknowledgment chahiye. Tumne unhe startle kiya, lekin agar hum shaant rahein to kuch bura nahi hoga.”

Bargad ki roots se ek low moan aaya — jaise hawa hollow bone se guzar rahi ho. Shapes form hone lagi — Mahadevi ke spirits se chhote, zyada fleeting, lekin moonlight mein visible. Woh boy aur Raghav ke gird swirl karne lage, phusphusati, uski energy ko tug kar rahe, recognition mang rahe.

Raghav ko ek important baat samajh aayi: ab sirf himmat kaafi nahi thi. Use understanding, remembrance, aur respect chahiye tha. Usne gehri saans li aur aloud bola:

“Main tumhe dekhta hoon. Main tumhe sunta hoon. Main tumhari kahaniyan yaad rakhunga. Main tumhare dard ko nahi bhoolne dunga.”

Figures ruk gaye, unke forms uncertainly flicker kar rahe the. Ek chhoti si shape — ek young girl hollow eyes wali, tattered dress mein — uske paas float hui. Usne phusphusaya: “Unhe batao… living logon ko batao ki unhone humse kya kiya…”

Raghav ne sar hila diya. “Main karunga. Main wada karta hoon. Tumhe ab darne ki zaroorat nahi.”

Hawa halki hone lagi, bachche ke arms par glowing symbols fade hone lage. Figures slowly grove mein recede kar gaye — visible lekin ab threatening nahi, unki phusphusahatein soft aur thankful.

Dadi paas aayi, Raghav ke kandhe par haath rakh diya. “Yeh sirf shuruaat hai. Gaon mein sorrow ke kayi layers hain, kayi forgotten fragments. Mahadevi sabse bada tha, lekin yeh… yeh chhoti injustices ke echoes hain, jo zyadatar logon ne notice nahi kiye. Tumhe unhe guide karna hoga, Raghav. Unhe witness karna. Innocent logon ko claim hone se bachana.”

Raghav ka jaw tight ho gaya. “Toh main… keeper hoon. Sirf Mahadevi ka nahi, balki in sab… fragments ka.”

“Haan,” Dadi ne quietly kaha. “Aur iske saath responsibility aati hai. Himmat tumhe dar se bachayegi. Samajh tumhe despair se bachayegi. Aur yaad rakhna… gaon ko bachayega.”

Jab hawa bamboo grove se phusphusati hui guzri, Raghav ko samajh aa gaya ki Kodipur ki shaanti fragile thi. Bargad shaant ho chuka tha, lekin gaon khud lingering grief se zinda tha. Har whisper, har flicker of light, ek yaad dilata tha ki memory, agar ignore ki gayi, kuch dangerous ban sakti hai.

Us raat, Raghav phir se bargad ke saamne khada tha, blackened wooden box haath mein. Uski nazar grove par thi jahan spirits recede ho chuke the. Kahin jadon ki gehrayi mein, ek faint pulse baaki tha — jaise ek heartbeat.

Ab use samajh aa gaya: Kodipur ke horrors sirf past tak limited nahi the. Woh zameen ki yaadon mein, injustice ke echoes mein, Mahadevi ke baad bache sorrow ke fragments mein zinda the. Aur woh… woh living aur dead ke beech ka bridge tha, wo awaaz jo unki kahaniyan aage le jaayegi.

Jab usne box band kiya, ek phusphusahat uske kaan se guzri — pehle se zyada soft: “Tum tayyar ho… baaki ke liye.”

Aur Raghav ko, ek jhatke ke saath, pata chala ki Kodipur ke baaki secrets abhi bhi wait kar rahe hain — patient, restless, aur eternal.

Chapter 7: The Unseen Reckoning

Raghav subah se pehle utha, gaon khamosh tha except for bamboo leaves ki faint rustling. Hawa thick thi, ek aisi presence se bhari hui jo woh mehsoos kar sakta tha lekin abhi tak nahi dekh pa raha tha. Kodipur mein kuch shift ho gaya tha — lingering spirits ki phusphusahatein pehle ke nights se zyada loud ho gayi thi, unease ka persistent undercurrent ban kar reh gayi thi.

Aaj, use apna pehla asli test face karna tha.

Jab woh bahar step kiya, to signs clear the. Doors apne aap khul rahi thi, lanterns flicker kar rahe the bina hawa ke, aur bargad ke aas-paas cold mist zameen se chipki hui thi. Raghav ka pulse tez ho gaya, lekin woh nahi ruka. Blackened wooden box ko chest se laga kar le gaya, jaise woh ek shield ho.

Grove se ek figure emerge hui — lambi, imposing shape, tattered robes mein, chehra shadow mein chhupa hua. Chhote spirits se alag, yeh presence defiance aur malice radiate kar rahi thi. Iske saath ki phusphusahatein harsh aur chaotic thi — Mahadevi ke mournful tones se alag: “Main yaad nahi rakhna chahta… main silence nahi honga… tum mujhe bind nahi kar sakte!”

Raghav ke haath kaanpte, lekin woh aage badha. “Main tumhe bind nahi karna chahta. Main sirf samajhna chahta hoon. Tumhari ek kahani hai. Main sununga.”

Figure ne hansi, ek hollow, bone-chilling sound jo grove mein echo hui. “Samajhna? Bewakoof ladka. Tum centuries ke betrayal ko nahi samajh sakte. Meri yaad dard hai. Mera gussa justice hai. Main tumhare words, promises, tumhari kamzor himmat se beyond hoon.”

Bargad ke neeche roots shift huye, jaise spirit ki fury ka jawab de rahe hon. Chhote flickers of light Raghav ke aas-paas erupt hue, aur mist thick ho kar usko icy embrace mein wrap kar gayi. Blackened box uske haath mein faintly pulse kar raha tha, jaise use act karne ke liye urge kar raha ho.

Raghav ne aankhen band ki, khud ko focus karne ki koshish ki. Use Dadi ke words yaad aaye: “Sirf himmat tumhe dar se bachayegi. Samajh tumhe despair se bachayegi. Aur yaad rakhna… gaon ko bachayega.”

Woh aloud bola, steady despite cold pressing from all sides: “Main tumhe acknowledge karta hoon. Main tumhare suffering ko dekhta hoon. Main tumhari kahani yaad rakhunga, chahe doosre na rakhein. Tumhara dard bhoola nahi gaya.”

Spirit thoda sa piche hata, uska form anger aur disbelief mein flicker kar raha tha. Phusphusahatein louder hui, ab words form karne lagi: “Tum dare karte ho… tum mere liye bolne ki himmat karte ho? Tum nahi kar sakte! Koi nahi kar sakta!”

Raghav ne wooden box khola, andar ki glowing symbols reveal ki. Usne carvings ko fingers se trace kiya, scroll ki rhythm ko follow karte hue. Pulsing light ne grove ko bhara, mist ko peeche dhakela, aur spirit ki hollow face ko illuminate kiya.

“Tum apne past se nahi chhip sakte,” Raghav ne continue kiya. “Tumhari kahani matter karti hai. Main use aage le jaunga. Main ensure karunga ki tumhara dard seen aur remembered ho. Lekin agar tum lash out karte raho, to tum forever trapped rahoge — ek fragment, lost aur gusse mein.”

Spirit ne cheekha, uski awaaz shattered glass jaisi. Roots violently writhed, aur zameen Raghav ke pairon ke neeche hili. Chhote spirits jo pehle guide kiye the, ab conflict ki wajah se uske gird swirl karne lage, unki phusphusahatein box ki pulse ke saath harmonize ho rahi thi.

Raghav ki himmat dube, lekin woh peeche nahi hata. Instead, usne spirit ki essence par focus kiya — uske rage ke neeche chhupa dard. “Main tumhe dekhta hoon,” usne phir kaha. “Main jaanta hoon tumne suffer kiya hai. Main yaad rakhunga. Tumhe kisi aur ko harm karne ki zaroorat nahi hai taaki tum sunwa sako.”

Lambe, tense pal ke liye, spirit still raha — jaise uske words consider kar raha ho. Phir, dheere-dheere, form soft hua. Hollow face intimidating se kam, zyada human ban gaya. Phusphusahatein badli — ab ek faint, mournful chorus: “Yaad rakho… bhoolo mat… hamari kahani le jaao…”

Raghav ne wooden box ki pulse steady aur strong mehsoos ki. Spirit raat mein fade hone lagi, sirf shimmering residue chhod kar — centuries ke anger aur sorrow ka faint trace.

Dadi silently uske peeche appear hui, aankhon mein relief aur pride dono. “Tumne keeper ke pehle asli challenge ko face kiya hai,” unhone softly kaha. “Sab spirits willingly acknowledgment accept nahi karenge. Kuch persuade kiye jaane chahiye, kuch tumhari himmat dekhna chahenge, aur kuch… apne hi sorrow ki sachchai ke saath confront kiye jaane chahiye.”

Raghav ke haath kaanpte the, lekin usne sar hila diya. “Main samajh gaya. Yeh… yeh sirf shuruaat hai.”

“Haan,” Dadi ne jawab diya. “Aur gaon tumhe aur test karega. Kodipur ke spirits kayi hain, aur sab patient nahi hain. Lekin tumne aaj raat apni worth prove kar di. Tumne seekh liya ki himmat ko samajh ke saath jodna padta hai, aur yaad rakhna sirf memory nahi — woh action hai.”

Jab raat ki hawa bamboo grove se phusphusati hui guzri, Raghav ko apne kaam ki enormity samajh aayi. Kodipur ki shaanti fragile thi, uska history soil aur hawa mein zinda tha. Use defiance aur gussa ka saamna kiya tha, lekin raat ne use sikha diya ki doosron ke dard ko acknowledge karna — chahe woh resist karein — true guardianship ka raasta hai.

Bargad ped khamosh khada tha, ek watchful sentinel, uski roots abhi bhi faintly pulse kar rahi thi un yaadon ke saath jo usne sambhale the. Aur kahin phusphusati hawa mein, awaazon ka chorus harmony mein murmur kar raha tha: “Tum tayyar ho… humein safe rakho…”

Raghav ne wooden box ko pakad liya, ab samajh gaya ki uska role keeper ke taur par sirf past yaad rakhna nahi — balki actively present ko restless echoes se guard karna hai.

Chapter 8: Secrets Buried in Blood

Suraj Kodipur par abhi-2 hi uga tha jab Raghav apne ancestral home ke attic mein khada tha. Dhool hawa mein frozen time ki tarah latki hui thi, aur sunlight stacks of yellowed papers, faded photos, aur rusted trunks par pad rahi thi. Dadi ne usse subah ek key di thi — jo generations se rakhi gayi thi, us din ke liye jab keeper ko sabse zyada zaroorat hogi.

“Yeh attic woh cheezein rakhta hai jo sau saalon se chhupayi gayi hain,” unhone kaha tha. “Sab pleasant nahi hain. Kuch truths heavy hain… lekin woh tumhare hain, aur tumhe unka saamna karna hoga.”

Raghav largest trunk ke paas gaya — metal corners corroded, lock long unused. Usne key slide ki, ghumayi, aur spine par ek jhatka mehsoos kiya jab lock khula. Andar letters, journals, aur parchments the — faded red ribbons se bandhe hue.

Usne pehla journal uthaya, leather cover brittle. Pages flip karte hue, use entries milin jo Kodipur ke decades before chronicled karti thi. Names jo use pata the — uske great-great-grandfather, ancestors jo gaon mein authority wield karte the — cruelty, betrayal, aur greed ke accounts ke saath likhe hue.

Ek entry ne use freeze kar diya:

"Spirits restless hain. Woh raat mein phusphusati hain, aur main unke gusse se darta hoon. Lekin hum unhe appease nahi kar sakte. Hum unhe bargad se bind karte hain apne liye protect karne ke liye, lekin kuch kabhi obey nahi karenge. Hamari bloodline cursed hai — jo mere baad aayenge, unhe burden uthana hoga."

Raghav ke haath kaanpte. Use pata tha ki uske family ke dark secrets hain, lekin journal ne sach reveal kiya: uske ancestors ne intentionally spirits ko bargad se bind kiya tha, use apne liye shield banaya tha jabki innocents ki atmaon ko condemn kiya. Yeh explain karta tha ki woh defiance kyun face kar raha tha — spirits sirf grieving nahi; woh centuries ke intentional injustice se enraged the.

Usne dusra journal uthaya — older aur zyada fragile. Entries shaky ink mein likhi thi, events chronicling kar rahi thi jo Kodipur ke current form se bhi pehle ke thi. Yahan ancestors Mahadevi ke baare mein baat kar rahe the — myth nahi, balki ek aurat jiska grief exploit kiya gaya, jiska sorrow capture kar ke power ka tool bana diya gaya.

Raghav ko apni lineage ka full horror samajh aaya: uska family sirf curse survive nahi kiya, balki usse profit bhi kiya, fear aur control ka cycle maintain kiya. Mahadevi ka spirit is wrong ka sabse bada manifestation tha, lekin chhote fragments jo use mile — bamboo grove ka ladka, tall defiant spirit — yeh sab same ancestral cruelty ke echoes the.

Aur jab woh aage padha, to uska seena tight ho gaya. Ek page par uske great-grandfather ka pact likha tha:

"Agar shraap zyada strong ho gaya, to hamari bloodline ka khoon use confront karega. Hum wooden box taiyar karenge, ek vessel jo chosen ko guide karega. Lekin jisne bhi falter kiya, uske liye bura hoga. Keeper ko grief endure karna hoga, warna sab kuch kho jayega."

Raghav ko samajh aa gaya ki woh kyun call kiya gaya tha. Woh sirf ek witness nahi tha — woh guilt aur horror mein forged responsibility ka inheritor tha. Uske ancestors ki silence aur greed ne restless spirits ka web create kiya tha, aur sirf wohi untangle kar sakta tha jo unka khoon carry karta tha.

Woh floor par baith gaya, centuries ka bojh uske upar daab raha tha. Uska role keeper ke taur par choice nahi — legacy thi, innocent logon ke suffering mein likhi hui. Jo whispers woh sun raha tha, woh sirf grief ke echoes nahi the — woh justice, acknowledgment, aur action ki demand thi us line se jo unke dard ka cause bani thi.

Dadi ki awaaz ne uske reverie ko toda — calm lekin firm. “Ab tum samajh gaye ho, Raghav. Spirits ki defiance random nahi hai. Yeh history bol rahi hai, accountability mang rahi hai. Tumhe ise confront karna hoga — gusse se nahi, samajh, himmat, aur yaad se.”

Raghav ne dheere se sar hila diya, chest mein resolve hardening. “Main karunga. Main unhe ignore nahi hone dunga. Main unka saamna karunga, har ek ka. Main unki kahaniyan le jaunga.”

Unhone usse ek purana amulet diya — wood se carved, blackened box jaisi symbols se etched. “Yeh pehle keeper ka tha. Yeh tumhe unki energy focus karne mein help karega, lekin yaad rakhna: tools courage ki jagah nahi le sakte. Spirits tumhe test karenge, tumhe apni limits tak push karenge. Woh truth mangenge.”

Raghav ne amulet neck par fasten kiya, ek subtle pulse mehsoos ki — connection Kodipur ke lingering echoes se. Bahar, bamboo morning breeze mein sway kar raha tha, aur use lagaa ki faint whispers sunai de rahi hain — almost jaise warning ho: “Khoon jaanta hai… khoon yaad rakhta hai… khoon ko act karna hoga…”

Woh khada hua, blackened box pakad kar, ancestors ke gunahon ka bojh shoulders par. Kodipur ke spirits ab raat ke mere shadows nahi the — woh history ka living testament the, acknowledgment mang rahe the. Aur Raghav ab samajh gaya tha ki uska journey keeper ke taur par sirf shuruaat tha.

Past ke horrors ne use ek purpose diya tha: confront karna, yaad rakhna, aur ensure karna ki har injustice, sorrow, aur gusse ka fragment uske through apni awaaz paaye.

Kodipur ki khamoshi fragile thi, aur uski yaadein zinda thi. Aur Raghav… Raghav sabka saamna karne ke liye tayyar tha.

Chapter 9: The Gathering of Echoes

Raaten jaldi aa gayi thi — thick aur oppressive, Kodipur ko ek darkness mein duba diya jaise tangible ho. Bargad ped, jo ab zyada tar khamosh rehta tha, faintly pulse kar raha tha — jaise convergence ko mehsoos kar raha ho. Raghav uske paas cautiously approach kiya, ek haath mein blackened wooden box, neck par amulet heartbeat-like rhythm se throb kar raha tha.

Aaj raat, phusphusahatein zyada strong, zyada insistent thi — multiple spirits se anger, grief, aur fear ke fragments le kar aayi thi. Grove ka ladka, tattered kapdon wali ladki, aur pehle wala tall defiant spirit wapas aaye the. Unke saath doosre bhi the — chhote, zyada fleeting, chehre sorrow se twist, movements restless.

Raghav bargad ke hollow mein step kiya, pulsing roots ne use guide kiya. Blackened box faintly glow kar raha tha, uske aas-paas ki energy ka respond kar raha tha. Amulet uski chest par throb kar raha tha, aur use laga jaise centuries ka bojh har direction se press kar raha ho.

“Tum aa gaye,” ek awaaz hissed — low aur menacing. Unfamiliar, lekin sharp, spirits ke murmur ko cut kar gayi. “Tum blood carry karte ho… lekin kya tum woh bardaasht kar sakte ho jo gaon maangta hai?”

Raghav ka dil ruk sa gaya. Use naya presence expect nahi tha, lekin instinct ne bataya ki yeh koi ordinary spirit nahi. Hawa thandi, denser ho gayi. Ek shadow grove se detach hui — lambi, patli figure, darkness mein lipat hui, aankhen burning coals jaisi. Uski phusphusahatein Raghav ke dimaag mein slither kar gayi: “Tum jahan belong nahi karte wahan interfere kar rahe ho… tum fail ho jaoge…”

Spirits ne is presence par react kiya — unki energy restless, chaotic. Raghav turant samajh gaya: yeh hidden antagonist tha, ancestral wrath ka ek fragment jo pacify hone se inkaar kar raha tha, ek spirit jo acknowledgment nahi, dominance chahta tha.

Raghav ne aankhen band ki aur aloud bola — awaaz steady despite tremor: “Main tumhe dekhta hoon. Main tumhare gusse ko samajhta hoon. Main tumhare suffering ko acknowledge karta hoon. Lekin violence aur dar tumhe peace nahi dega. Tumhe mujh par bharosa karna hoga, kyunki main unki yaad carry karta hoon jo tumhe wrong kiya.”

Shadow ne hansi — cracking stone jaisi awaaz. “Trust? Tum fear ko command nahi kar sakte, ladke. Tum forgotten logon se respect nahi maang sakte!”

Raghav ne gehri saans li, wooden box ki rhythm ko follow karte hue. Usne carefully box khola, glowing symbols ko trace kiya, spirits ki energy se connect hua. Ek-ek kar ke, usne names bola, grievances phusphusayi, aur ancestral journals se uncover kiye gaye truths recite kiye.

“Tumhara suffering ignore nahi kiya gaya. Main ise le jaunga. Main ise honor karunga. Tumhe living logon ko destroy karne ki zaroorat nahi hai taaki tum yaad raho,” usne intoned — awaaz steady aur firm.

Chhote spirits shaant hone lage, unki energy box ki glowing pulse ke saath harmonize hone lagi. Lekin shadow resist kar raha tha — violently swirl kar raha tha, uski energy grove mein storm ki tarah cut kar rahi thi. Roots writhed aur snap huye, aur hawa electricity se charged lag rahi thi.

Raghav ke haath kaanpte, lekin usne strength untruths se li — jo usne uncover ki thi. “Main samajhta hoon tum kyun resist kar rahe ho,” usne shadow se directly kaha. “Tum centuries ke betrayal se bound ho. Tum maante ho ki living log atone nahi kar sakte. Lekin main yahan hoon. Main unka khoon hoon. Main keeper hoon. Main woh memory carry kar sakta hoon jo tum release nahi kar sakte.”

Shadow ruka, burning coal jaisi aankhen uncertainty mein flicker kar rahi thi. Chhote spirits ki phusphusahatein hollow ko bhari, Raghav ke words ke saath chorus ban kar resonate kar rahi thi. “Yaad rakho… yaad rakho… yaad rakho…”

Pehli baar, antagonist ne hesitate kiya. Grove ki energy slightly shift hui — kam chaotic, roots box ke rhythm mein pulse karne lage. Raghav ne ek tenuous lekin real connection mehsoos ki — apne aur defiant spirit ke beech.

“Main nahi dubunga,” usne firmly kaha. “Tumhe bhoola nahi jaayega, lekin na hi living log tumhare dard ki wajah se suffer karenge. Hum balance find kar sakte hain. Tum apna gussa release kar sakte ho, jaise Mahadevi ne kiya, aur fragments jo tum pakde hue ho, rest payenge.”

Ek tense silence — sirf bamboo leaves ki faint rustle ne toda. Phir, dheere-dheere, shadow dissolve ho gaya — ek faint shimmering residue chhod kar, jaise embers jo fire ke baad cool ho rahe hon. Chhote spirits Raghav ke gird ek baar phir circle karne lage, gratitude phusphusati, unki energy finally harmonize ho gayi.

Dadi grove ke kinare appear hui, aankhon mein relief aur pride. “Tumne keeper ki authority ka pehla asli test face kiya hai. Spirits ko tum par bharosa seekhna hoga, aur tumhe unhe guide karna seekhna hoga. Kuch resist karenge, kuch lash out karenge — lekin har encounter tumhe gaon ki memory se bond strengthen karega.”

Raghav ki saans deep aur uneven aa rahi thi. “Yeh… maine socha se zyada mushkil hai. Gussa… defiance… mujhe almost consume kar gaya.”

“Haan,” Dadi ne softly kaha. “Lekin himmat, samajh, aur yaad rakhna gusse se zyada strong hai. Yaad rakho, Raghav: spirits tumhari bravery ko test nahi karte — tumhari resolve, tumhari empathy, tumhari ability test karte hain ki history ko carry karo bina uske wrath ka hissa bano.”

Bargad ped lamba khada tha, roots abhi bhi faintly pulse kar rahi thi. Kodipur abhi ke liye khamosh tha, lekin Raghav jaanta tha ki yeh raat sirf shuruaat thi. Aur fragments baaki the, aur truths buried thi, aur defiance confront karna tha. Lekin pehli baar, use quiet assurance mehsoos hui ki woh jo bhi shadows mein reh gaya ho, uska saamna kar sakta hai.

Kahin phusphusati hawa mein, awaazon ka chorus softly murmur kar raha tha: “Hum yaad rakhe gaye… hum dekhe gaye… aur tum… tum humein guide karoge.”

Raghav ne blackened box ko pakad liya, ab poori tarah samajh gaya ki uska role ab reactive nahi tha. Woh bridge tha, awaaz thi, aur Kodipur ke restless memories ka guardian. Aur ab, pehle se zyada, gaon ko use unwavering chahiye tha.

Chapter 10: The Stirring of the Living

Kodipur confusion mein jaagaa. Doors apne aap khul rahi thi, jars shelves se gir rahe the, aur livestock panic mein bhaag rahe the. Narrow lanes mein whispers travel kar rahi thi — indistinct lekin urgent — villagers ko bamboo grove ki taraf kheench rahi thi.

Raghav ne disturbance ko bahar nikalne se pehle hi mehsoos kar liya. Blackened wooden box uske haath mein violently throb kar raha tha — uski dhadkan erratic, warning de rahi thi ki kuch previous encounters se alag ho chuka hai. Hawa mein sirf spirits ki awaazein nahi — villagers ki bhi thi, twisted, reality se confuse.

Square tak pahunchte hue, Raghav ne source dekha: bargad ke roots ke upar ek faint, shimmering figure hover kar rahi thi — uski presence malice radiate kar rahi thi. Doosre spirits se alag, yeh living logon ko touch kar rahi thi — unke dimaag mein slip kar rahi thi, fear aur confusion stir kar rahi thi. Villagers sleepwalking jaise latk rahe the, ek doosre par threats aur accusations bol rahe the, unki aankhen unnatural light se glazed.

Dadi Shanta uske paas appear hui, chehra serious. “Yeh living logon ko manipulate karti hai, beta. Doubt, gusse, aur dar par feed karti hai. Agar yeh persist karegi, to gaon ko andar se fracture kar degi.”

Raghav ne blackened box ko tight pakda. “Main ise kaise rokun?”

“Tumhe jaldi act karna hoga. Tum ise sirf force se nahi lad sakte,” Dadi ne kaha. “Tumhe apni presence assert karni hogi keeper ke taur par — dono, spirits aur living ko clarity ki taraf guide karna hoga.”

Raghav bargad ki taraf badha, possessed villagers aur figure ke beech position liya. Box ki chamak brightly pulse kar rahi thi, spirit ke gird swirl kar rahi mist ko illuminate kar rahi thi. Uski awaaz Raghav ke dimaag mein slither kar gayi: “Woh darne wale hain… woh suffer karenge… main tumse zyada strong hoon… unse bhi…”

Raghav ne saans steady ki. Use previous spirits ke lessons yaad aaye: himmat, samajh, yaad rakhna. Usne box par focus kiya, uski energy ko chest ke amulet ke saath resonate hone diya, aur aloud bola:

“Main keeper hoon. Main tumhe acknowledge karta hoon, aur main villagers ko bhi acknowledge karta hoon. Tum unhe control nahi kar sakti, aur mujhe bhi nahi. Apna grasp release karo, warna tum forever unseen aur unheard rahogi.”

Figure ne cheekha — ek sound jo raat ko shattered glass ki tarah tear kar gayi. Villagers violently sway karne lage, ab unki phusphusahatein ek doosre par accusations ban kar scream kar rahi thi. Dar ne Raghav ke seenay ko pakad liya, lekin use khud ko shaant rakhna pada — box ki pulse ko spirits aur living ki energy ke saath synchronize hone diya.

“Tum doubt par feed karti ho,” usne firmly continue kiya. “Lekin main sachchai ki yaad carry karta hoon. Main nahi dubunga. Main tumhe dekhta hoon, aur main unhe bhi dekhta hoon. Aaj raat tumhare gusse se koi nuksaan nahi hoga.”

Figure thoda sa piche hati, uska form flicker kar raha tha. Bargad ke gird ki mist violently twist hui, phir dheere-dheere settle hone lagi. Villagers ne blink kiya — unki glazed aankhen clear hui jab influence kam hua. Kuch zameen par baith gaye, kaanpte hue, lekin spirit ki manipulation se azad.

Raghav ne pressure badhaya, box ko aur khola. Light patterns mein pour hui — amulet ke symbols ke saath resonate kar rahi thi — Kodipur ke spirits aur living ki energies ko connect kar rahi thi. Figure ne howl kiya — frustration mein zyada, dar mein kam — aur ek tense pal ke liye, lagaa jaise spirit breakthrough karne wali ho.

Phir, Raghav ne unwavering authority se bola: “Tum sorrow aur gusse ki wajah se exist karti ho, lekin tumhara raasta yahan khatam hota hai. Tum living logon ko nuksaan nahi pahunchaogi. Tum acknowledgment ke through peace payogi, control ke through nahi.”

Spirit ka form collapse hone laga — malice soft, shimmering residue mein dissolve hui. Peeche chhodi gayi phusphusahatein ab threatening nahi thi — sorrow ki faint murmurs: “Yaad rakho… release karo… acknowledge karo…”

Villagers poore taur par stir hone lage — confusion se around dekh rahe the, lekin unhurt. Raghav ne saans chhodi — uske chehre par pasina beh raha tha, box ki pulse thamti ja rahi thi jaise threat recede ho raha ho.

Dadi uske paas aayi, kandhe par haath rakh diya. “Tumne ek aisi spirit ka saamna kiya jo living logon ko manipulate karna chahti thi. Yeh tumhari himmat aur command dono ka test hai keeper ke taur par. Spirits jo living ko touch karti hain, sabse dangerous hoti hain — kyunki woh weaknesses exploit karti hain aur dar failati hain. Tumne pass kar liya, lekin yaad rakho: aise challenges intensity mein badhenge.”

Raghav ne sar hila diya, exhaustion uske upar bhaari bojh ban kar aa raha tha. “Living vulnerable hain, aur main… main unhe protect karna chahiye. Responsibility… maine socha bhi nahi tha itni bhaari hogi.”

“Haan,” Dadi ne softly kaha, grove ko scan karte hue. “Lekin tum seekh rahe ho, aur har encounter ke saath tum strong ho rahe ho. Kodipur ki yaadein kayi hain, Raghav. Kuch sorrow se chipki hain, kuch gusse se, kuch injustice se. Tumhe sabko guide karna hoga, warna gaon khud unki unrest mein succumb ho jayega.”

Bargad ped faintly pulse kar raha tha — jaise uski victory acknowledge kar raha ho. Bahar, villagers apni composure regain kar rahe the — unaware ki unka narrow escape hua tha. Lekin Raghav sach jaanta tha: living aur restless spirits ke beech ki line patli, fragile, aur constantly tested thi.

Jab raat gehri hui, usne blackened box ko tight pakda, khud se phusphusaya: “Main unhe protect karunga. Main yaad rakhunga. Main unhe guide karunga… sabko.”

Aur kahin, phusphusati hawa mein, awaazon ka chorus faintly echo kar raha tha: “Tum keeper ho… aur hum tumhare saath chalenge.”

—

Chapter 11: The Vessel Among Us

Agali shaam, Kodipur par ek strange unease settle ho gayi thi. Villagers nervously phusphus rahe the, ek doosre ko dekh rahe the — jaise koi invisible presence se dartey hon. Raghav ne barely sota tha — mehsoos kar raha tha ki aaj raat ka disturbance bargad ya fragments se nahi aayega — balki kahin zyada close se… village ke andar se.

Woh square ki taraf chala, haath mein blackened wooden box, neck par amulet faintly pulse kar raha tha. Grove ke paas ke small temple se guzarte waqt, usne doorway se unnatural light ka ek flicker notice kiya. Andar jhaankte hi, uski saans ruk gayi.

Temple ke center mein, ek young woman kneel kar rahi thi — aankhen roll back, body violently kaanp rahi thi. Shadows ke wisps uske gird swirl kar rahe the — Kodipur ke past ke human figures ki shape le rahe the. Woh names mutter kar rahi thi — kuch familiar, kuch unknown — uski awaaz spirits ki phusphusahaton se overlap ho rahi thi.

Raghav ne cautiously step liya. “Ruko! Tum unhe channel kar rahi ho… tum unhe yahan le aa rahi ho!”

Woman ki aankhen snap open hui — apne actions ka koi recognition nahi. Uska body convulse hua, aur shadows uske form se leap karke village ki taraf bhag gaye — haunting shapes mein twist hote hue, jinhe dekh kar koi bhi terrify ho jata. Spirits, jo pehle uski guidance follow karti thi, ab uske peeche chal rahi thi — uske fear aur grief ko amplify kar rahi thi.

Dadi silently uske paas appear hui. “Woh unaware hai,” unhone phusphusaya. “Kuch living vessels ban sakte hain. Restless fragments unki taraf attract hote hain, emotion par feed karte hain. Agar guide nahi kiye gaye, to woh dangerous ho sakte hain — sirf spirits ke liye nahi, living ke liye bhi.”

Raghav ka dil tez dhadak raha tha. Use chaos failta dikha — villagers trip kar rahe, stumble kar rahe, panic mein phusphus rahe. Yeh previous threats se alag tha — is baar danger village ke andar se aa raha tha, ek living conduit jo spirits ki unrest ko amplify kar raha tha.

Woh slowly approach kiya, blackened box raise kiya. “Main keeper hoon. Main yahan hoon tumhe aur gaon ko protect karne ke liye. Tum safe ho, lekin mujh par bharosa karo.”

Young woman ne involuntarily cheekha — shadows violently react kar rahe the, jaise uski authority sense kar rahe hon. Raghav ko samajh aa gaya — direct confrontation uski state ko aur kharab kar dega. Use uske dimaag tak pahunchana tha, awareness tak — bina spirits ko frighten kiye jo usse chipki hui thi.

Aankhen band karke, usne box ki pulse par focus kiya — uski energy ko amulet ke saath resonate hone diya. Softly phusphusaya — words of acknowledgment aur reassurance — dono, woman aur surrounding spirits ke liye.

“Tum akeli nahi ho,” usne gently kaha. “Fragments recognition mang rahe hain, harm nahi. Main unki yaad carry karunga, aur tumhe unki awaaz banne ki zaroorat nahi. Tum safe ho. Woh bhi safe hain. Hum unhe saath mein guide karenge.”

Dheere-dheere, shadows settle hone lage — box ki pulse aur uski calm authority se attract hoke. Woman ka kaanpna kam hua, aur woh floor par collapse kar gayi — exhausted lekin unhurt. Raghav uske paas baith gaya, apne focus ko unki energy par rakhte hue.

“Main…” woman ne weakly phusphusaya. “Main… nahi jaanti kya hua. Mainne… unhe mehsoos kiya. Main ise rok nahi saki.”

“Tum ek vessel thi,” Raghav ne gently explain kiya. “Kuch living spirits ko channel kar sakte hain, lekin yeh tumhara fault nahi. Woh acknowledgment, guidance, aur samajh mang rahe the. Isliye woh tumhare paas aaye. Main balance restore kar chuka hoon, lekin tumhe shaant rehna hoga, aur ab se main unhe guide karunga.”

Dadi doorway se sar hila rahi thi. “Living spirits jitne fragile hote hain, utne hi. Lekin yeh bhi ek lesson hai: threats hamesha external nahi hote. Gaon khud past ke echoes ko contain karta hai, aur kuch vessels ban jate hain — knowingly ya unknowingly. Tumhe vigilant rehna hoga, aur dono sides ko protect karna hoga.”

Raghav ne woman ko uthaya, blackened box uske haath mein steadily pulse kar raha tha. Bahar, gaon shant lag raha tha — unrest fade ho raha tha, though woh jaanta tha ki yeh temporary hai. Kuch spirits abhi bhi resist karenge, aur aur vessels aa sakte hain. Lekin abhi ke liye, balance restore ho chuka tha, aur immediate danger khatam.

Usne woman ko dekha — uski aankhon mein faint shimmer — spirits ki remnants jo usne carry ki thi. “Tum theek ho jaogi,” usne softly kaha. “Main tumhe guide karunga, aur saath mil kar hum Kodipur ko safe rakhenge.”

Hawa bamboo grove se phusphusati hui guzri — spirits ki gratitude ki faint echoes le kar. Raghav ko ek heavy responsibility settle hui — pehle se bhi zyada bhaari. Gaon, uske spirits, aur uske living — sab connected the — ways jo woh abhi samajhna shuru kar raha tha.

Kodipur ki shaanti abhi bhi nazuk thi, aur zindagi aur yaadon ke beech ki lakeer pehle se bhi zyada patli ho chuki thi. Lekin Raghav ek cheez mein bilkul sure tha: chahe kuch bhi ho jaaye, woh na toh zinda logon ko nuksaan hone dunga, na hi murda aatmaon ko.

Hawa ki fufkar mein kahin, aatmaen dheemi awaaz mein kah rahi thi:

“You see us… you protect us… and you carry our story.”

Raghav ne kaale lakdi ka box kas ke pakda — use pata tha ki sabse khatarnak challenges hamesha wo nahi hote jo nazar aate hain… aur yeh bhi ki zinda log bhi kabhi-kabhi masaazi ke gusse ke bejaan agents ban ja sakte hain.

Chapter 12: The Forgotten Altar

Kodipur kabhi itna chup nahi raha tha. Bamboo grove bhi, jo hamesha bechain aatmaon ki fufkaron se jee raha hota tha, ab saans bhi rok ke baitha lag raha tha. Raghav jaanta tha — aaj raat jawab laayegi… aur khatre bhi.

Dadi Shanta use gaon ke kinare ek patli, jhadiyon se bhari path par le gayi. “Bahut kam logon ko yaad hai ye jagah,” unhone dheere se kaha, awaaz thodi si kaanpti hui. “Ye sthal Kodipur se bhi purana hai, peepal ke ped se bhi zyada purana. Hamare poorvaj yahan aatmaon — achchi ya badmash — dono ko bandhne ke liye rituals perform karte the. Lekin waqt ke saath, ye gyaan kho gaya… bhoola diya gaya… aur chhod diya gaya, jisse wo beemar ho gaye.”

Raghav peeche chalta raha, kaale lakdi ke box ko pakde hue. Har kadam ke saath hawa bhaari ho rahi thi — agarbatti ki halki khushboo, sadaapn ki mehak, aur kuch bahut purana… kuch zinda. Uske seenay par amulet dhadak raha tha, apni dhadkan tez kar raha tha, jaise mitti ke neeche chhupi taakat ko mehsoos kar raha ho.

Aakhir mein, woh ek kholi mein pahunche, jisme ek dhool aur kaii se dhaka, purane nishaanon se taraashaa hua pathar ka chabootra tha. Aas-paas ke pedon ki jaden iske kinaron tak phail chuki thi, jaise prakriti ne ise wapas apne paas le liya ho.

Dadi chabootre ke paas ghutne tek kar baitheen, sadion ke gande aur mitthi ko saaf karte hue. “Is chabootre ka istemaal hamari zameen par bhatakte aatmaon ko control karne ke liye hota tha. Lekin waqt ke saath, ye gyaan kho gaya. Ab ye sthal fragments ka center ban gaya hai. Woh yahan ikatthe hote hain… lekin be-kaboo, gusse mein, aur anjaane.”

Raghav aage badha, uske rongte khade ho gaye. Jab usne chabootre ke kinare ko chhua, toh ek jhatka uske shareer mein daud gaya, aur kholi mein chhaayein hilne lagi. Wo fragments jo usne pehle guide kiye the — ladka, ladki, lambi aur juratmand aatma — sab wahan dikhai diye, chabootre ke gird ghumte hue, unki shaklein bechain aur asahaj.

Chabootre se ek gambhir, goonjti hui awaaz uthi, purani aur hakimana: “Khoon wapas aaya hai… rakshak wapas aaya hai… kya tum humein command karoge ya khud hi nasht ho jaoge?”

Raghav ne kaala box ooncha uthaya, uski roshni ne kholi ko bhar diya. “Main rakshak hoon,” usne dhardharta se kaha. “Main tumhari yaadein, tumhari kahaniyan, tumhare gila carry karta hoon. Main tumhe guide karunga, nuksaan nahi pahunchaunga. Tum sunaye jaoge, aur zinda log safe rahenge.”

Fragments ne use test kiya — unki energy be-tarteeb, lagbhag overwhelm karne wali. Fufkar aur cheekhein hawa mein ghumne lagi, chabootre ki goonj ke saath mil kar. Raghav ko apne poorvajon ki virasat ka poora bojh ehsaas hua: ye sthal ek zamaney mein control ka tool tha, aur ab ye ek faisla sunane wali jagah ban chuka tha.

Usne box par banaye nishaanon ko trace kiya, unki energy ko chabootre ke nishaanon se jodta gaya. Dheere-dheere chaos harmonize hone laga, fragments ki shaklein stable hui, unki awaazein komal hui. Lekin kholi ke kinare ek chhaaya reh gayi — ek aisi maujoodgi jo pehle kabhi nahi dekhi thi, baaki sab se zyada andheri, jisme virodh ki lau thi.

Dadi ne kaha, “Ye woh akhri fragment hai jo yahan bandh tha. Ye control se nafrat karti hai, tumse bhi. Ho sakta hai ye aasan se jhukne wali na ho.”

Raghav ne dar ko neeche nigaal liya. “Main control nahi chahta,” usne zor se kaha. “Main sirf pehchan chahta hoon. Tumhara dard sach hai. Tumhari kahani yaad rakhayi jaayegi. Lekin tumhe mujhe guide karne dena hoga, warna tum hamesha gusse mein kho jaoge.”

Chhaaya ruki, phir aage badhi, uski himmat test karne. Zameen se jaden nikal kar uske gird serpents ki tarah ghoomne lagi. Raghav ne box ko kas ke pakda, amulet ki dhadkan par focus kiya, apni energy ko fragments ke saath sync kiya.

“Main yaadon ko carry karta hoon,” usne har shabd ko ghanti ki tarah bajate hue dohraaya. “Main yaad rakhunga. Tumhe kisi ko nuksaan pahunchane ki zaroorat nahi. Apna gussa chhod do, aur pehchan ke zariye shaanti paao, himmat se nahi.”

Chhaaya ruki, phir dheere-dheere ek halke chamak mein ghul gayi, jo baaki fragments mein merge ho gayi. Kholi chup ho gayi — sirf bamboo ke patton ki halki sarsarahat aur box ki steady dhadkan sunai deti thi.

Dadi ne uske kandhe par haath rakha. “Tumne altar ki yaadon ko jagaya bina uske qaid mein aa’e. Ye koi chhoti baat nahi. Lekin yaad rakhna, Raghav: ye sthal tumhe spirits ke saath bond ko majboot kar sakta hai, ya tumhe tabaah kar sakta hai agar galat istemaal kiya gaya. Tumhe hamesha alert rehna hoga, apne iraade par nazar rakhna hoga.”

Raghav ne saans chhodi — thakan thi, lekin ek nayi zimmewari ka ehsaas bhi. Usne samjha ki Kodipur ki history zinda hai, aur ye bhoola hua altar sirf power ki jagah nahi — ye ek test hai, ek yaad dilata hai ki balance aur chaos ke beech ki lakeer kitni nazuk hai.

Jab woh khada hua, fragments ne use akhri baar gher liya, dheemi fufkar ke saath: “Hum dekhe gaye… hum sunaye gaye… aur tum… tum humein carry karte ho.”

Kaale lakdi ka box uske haathon mein halki si roshni bikherta raha. Raghav jaanta tha — ye sirf shuruaat hai. Kodipur ke paas kayi raaz hain, kayi paraton ki pertein, aur kayi aur challenges. Lekin pehli baar, usne apni zimmewari ko bojh nahi, balki ek aisi duty samjhi jo woh nibha sakta hai.

Raati ki hawa ne altar se fufkarte hue kaha, jaise ek wada ho: “Guide karo humein… hum tumhare saath chalenge.”

—

Chapter 13: The Spirit of the Forgotten King

Kodipur ke upar ki hawa bhaari thi, intezaar se bhari hui. Raat chup thi, lekin ek bechain energy ne niche tak apni goonj faila rakhi thi, jaise gaon khud saans rok ke baitha ho. Raghav chowk se guzar raha tha, kaale lakdi ke box ko pakde hue — uski roshni kam thi lekin steady. Uska dimaag abhi bhi bhule hue altar ke waqiyat mein uljha hua tha, lekin Dadi ne use chetaya tha: bade challenges aa rahe hain.

Bamboo grove ke kinare se ek thandi maujoodgi ugg nikli. Jo fragments aur vessels usne dekhe the, unse bilkul alag — ye spirit mein adhikar aur taakat thi. Uski shakal shaan-daar thi, purane shahi kapdon ke tukdon se dhaki hui, sar par ek katte hue metal ka taj thoda sa tilta hua. Pighalti hui sonay jaisi aankhein gaon ko nafrat se dekh rahi thi.

Raghav tham gaya. Usne purani journals padhe the — ek bhule hue ruler ka zikr tha, ek raja jiski zulm ne kayi aatmaon ko Kodipur se jod diya tha. Lekin usne kabhi socha nahi tha ki seedha saamna hoga.

“Main Raja Devendra hoon,” spirit ne kaha, uski awaaz raat mein garajti hui. “Ek waqt main is zameen ka malik tha, aur mera hukum hi qanoon tha. Tum meri cheezon par command karne ki himmat karte ho? Tum… mere gaddaron ki santan?”

Raghav ne box ko zor se pakda. “Main yahan tumse takkar lene nahi aaya hoon,” usne sthir swar mein kaha. “Main rakshak hoon. Main un logo ki yaadein carry karta hoon jo zulm ka shikaar hue, jo bol nahi sakte. Tumhara gussa ma’loom hai. Tumhari kahani yaad rakhayi jaayegi — lekin zinda logon ko nuksaan nahi pahunchega.”

Spirit ne hansa — pathar pe pathar ghiste hone jaisi awaaz. “Zinda log? Woh mere neeche hain. Unki takleef mujhe maza deti hai. Woh mera ilaaka hain, aur tum decide nahi kar sakte ki main kya kar sakta hoon. Tum… jhuk jaoge ya toot jaoge.”

Raghav ki dhadkan tez hui, lekin usne Dadi ke shabd yaad kiye: himmat, samajh, yaad rakhna. Usne box khola aur chamakte nishaanon ko trace kiya, pehle guide kiye hue fragments se connect hua, unki collective energy ko apne andar kheecha.

“Tum yahan ab raj nahi karte,” usne firm awaaz mein kaha. “Tumhari kahani Kodipur ki yaadon ka hissa hai, lekin iska matlab yeh nahi ki tum zinda logon par raj kar sakte ho. Main tumhe guide karunga, aur gaon tumhara sach jaanega. Yehi balance hai jo tum dhoondh rahe ho, dominance nahi.”

Spirit aage badhi, purane sipahiyon ki chhaayen uske gird materialize hui, bechaini ki ek fauj ban gayi. Jaden zameen se nikal kar uske gird lashon ki tarah marne lagi, aur hawa violent energy se dhadakne lagi. Raghav ne sadion ka bojh mehsoos kiya, lekin woh khada raha, box ki dhadkan ko fragments ke saath sync karne diya.

“Tum itni himmat se history ke liye bol rahe ho!” raja ki aatma ne garaj kar kaha. “Tum, jo meri hi bloodline se bandhe ho, phir bhi kamzor! Tum fail ho jaoge, aur Kodipur mujhe apna malik yaad karega, tumhe nahi!”

Raghav ka dimaag tezi se daud raha tha. Fragments use gher rahe the, apni energy de rahe the, ek saath fufkar rahe the: “Hum yaad karte hain… hum dekhte hain… hum tumhare saath hain…” Usne mehsoos kiya ki spirits ka bharosa hi uski sabse majboot weapon hai. Unki collective yaad, unka usko acknowledge karna — yehi unrest ko stabilize kar sakta hai.

“Main tumhari taakat ko deny nahi karta,” Raghav ne box ko ooncha uthate hue kaha. “Lekin dar ke zariye authority legacy nahi banati. Sachchi yaad pehchan, insaaf aur balance se aati hai. Tum zinda logon ko nuksaan nahi pahunchaoge. Tum darr nahi failaoge. Main tumhari kahani carry karta hoon — aur gaon use samman dega, uske qaid mein nahi.”

Ek tanav bhari pal ke liye, spirit hichki khayi — uski pighalti hui aankhein mein bewaqoofi thi. Sipahiyon ki chhaayen thodi si ghul gayin, jaden naram hui, aur hawa ka dabav kam hua.

“Tum… mujhe challenge karne ki himmat karte ho…” Raja Devendra ne kaha, uski awaaz mein gussa aur hairat dono. “Meri bloodline ka khoon… mujhe command kar raha hai… aur phir bhi tum…”

Raghav ki awaaz clear aur be-doubt thi: “Main rakshak hoon. Main masaazi ke liye past ko carry karta hoon. Tumhara gussa, tumhara dard, tumhari yaad — sab carry kiye jaayenge, lekin nuksaan ke bina. Tum yaad rakhaye jaoge… sahi tareeke se.”

Spirit piche hati, phir dheere-dheere sonay jaisi chamak mein ghul gayi, apni purani taakat ki goonj chhod kar. Wo fragments jo usne guide kiye the, use gher kar quiet acknowledgment mein ghoomne lage, ab unki fufkar ek saath harmonize ho chuki thi: “Tum humein dekhte ho… tum humein guide karte ho… aur hum yaad karte hain.”

Dadi chhaayon se nikli, chehra gambhir lekin proud. “Tumne gaon ki sabse andheri history se judi sabse taakatwar aatma ka saamna kiya hai. Kam log aise confrontation mein zinda bach paaye hain. Yaad rakhna, Raghav: har spirit jo tum saamne karte ho, ek lesson carry karta hai, Kodipur ka ek sach. Jinke zyada strong hote hain, unke liye tumhari samajh bhi utni hi gehri honi chahiye.”

Raghav ne saans chhodi — kaala box uske haathon mein steady dhadak raha tha. Usne mehsoos kiya ki uski zimmewari exponential badh chuki hai. Zinda aur murda, history aur present — sab ek doosre se juda hain, aur woh hi thread hai jo inhe balance mein rakhta hai.

Jab hawa ne bamboo grove mein sarasaraahat machayi, fragments ki halki fufkar goonji: “Rakshak worthy hai… rakshak dekhta hai… rakshak humein carry karta hai.”

Raghav ne raat ke aasmaan ki taraf dekha — ab usko poori tarah samajh aa gaya tha ki Kodipur ka masaal zinda hai, uski goonj pehchan maang rahi hai, aur uski journey abhi shuru hui hai.

Chapter 14: The Betrayer in Our Midst

Kodipur par subah dhabbi si thi, suraj ki roshni jaise dar kar aayi ho, gaon ki bechain energy se bachti hui. Raghav ne raat bhar Raja Devendra ki aatma aur guide kiye hue fragments ke baare mein socha tha. Lekin jab woh chowk se guzra, toh ek ajeeb si bechaini uske rongton tak chadh gayi. Kuch galat tha — spirits nahi, balki zinda log.

Fufkar us tak pahunchi, pehle kuch dikhe. Gaon walay dheemi awaazon mein baat kar rahe the, purane kuein ki taraf dekh rahe the. Deewaron se chhaayen chipki hui thi, suraj ke bawajood bhi hil rahi thi. Kaala box uske haathon mein halka sa dhadak raha tha — warning de raha tha ki ye unrest alag hai.

Dadi Shanta chupke se uske paas aayi. “Raghav,” unhone kaha, awaaz dheemi aur gambhir. “Sab khatre masaazi se judhe nahi hote. Kuch greed, ambition… ya jealousy se paida hote hain. Ek aisa shaks hai zinda logon mein jo is unrest ko apne faide ke liye harness karna chahta hai.”

Raghav ne brows tight kiye. “Kaun?”

Dadi ki nazar chowk par ghum rahi thi. “Koi trusted. Koi jo dar aur dard samajhta hai, lekin use power samajhta hai. Agar hum intervene nahi karenge, toh Kodipur tabaah ho sakta hai — spirits se nahi, balki un zinda logon se jo unhe summon kar rahe hain.”

Woh use purane kuein ke paas ek tangi gali ki taraf le gayi, jahan ek aadmi jhuka hua tha, dheere se mantra padh raha tha, haathon se zameen par nishaan banata hua. Uske gird chhaayen ghoom rahi thi — wo fragments jo Raghav ne pehle guide kiye the, ab unnaturally us aadmi ki taraf kheench rahe the.

Raghav ki dhadkan tez hui. “Woh unka istemaal kar raha hai… manipulate kar raha hai. Woh… deliberately summon kar raha hai.”

Aadmi ki nazar ekdam se Raghav par padi — thandi aur calculate karti hui. “Ah… young keeper,” usne kaha, awaaz smooth lekin shararat se bhari. “Tumhare baare mein suna hai. Masaazi ka bojh utha rahe ho… zinda logon ko protect karne ki koshish kar rahe ho. Kitna noble.”

Raghav aage badha, kaala box pakde hue. “Isko band karo. Tum nahi samajhte kya kar rahe ho. Spirits power ke tools nahi hain. Woh yaadein hain, dard hain, insaaf hain — woh tumhare nahi hain.”

Aadmi ne hansa — ek low, sinister sound. “Oh, main bilkul samajhta hoon. Mujhe power chahiye. Kodipur ki unrest meri influence ki raah hai. Spirits unki sunte hain jo dar command karte hain, nahi jo unhe pamper karte hain. Tum? Tum kamzor ho. Tum dekhte ho ki woh kya hain, nahi ki woh tumhare liye kya kar sakte hain.”

Fragments tezi se ghoomne lage, unki energy chaotic ho gayi aadmi ke manipulation ke under. Paas khade gaon walay piche hatne lage, kuch dar kar bhaag gaye. Raghav ke seenay par amulet urgent dhadak raha tha, box ki roshni tez ho gayi jaise use action lene ko keh rahi ho.

“Tum unhe nuksaan nahi pahunchaoge,” Raghav ne alley mein goonjti hui awaaz mein kaha. “Main rakshak hoon. Main pehchan command karta hoon, dar nahi. Unhe abhi release karo.”

Aadmi ki muskurahat bigad gayi. “Aur agar main mana kar doon?”

Raghav ne aankhein band ki, pehle guide kiye hue fragments ki energy ko andar kheenchta hua. Woh uske allies the, uske tools nahi. Unki fufkar ne support ka chorus banaya: “Hum yaad karte hain… hum trust karte hain… hum tumhare saath hain…”

Aankhein khol kar, usne kaala box ooncha uthaya. Usme se roshni ne patterns mein flow kiya jo amulet se resonate kar rahi thi, summoned fragments ke chaos mein weave hoti gayi. Chhaayen ne resistance ki, bahar ki taraf lashen mari, lekin spirits ki collective energy ne Raghav ke intent ke saath harmony banayi.

Aadmi chillaya jab spirits ne piche hatna shuru kiya — unki shaklein stable hui, unki energy ne apna asli balance wapas pa liya. Zameen par jo nishaan woh banaya tha, woh halka sa jal kar raakh mein badal gaya, fragments ne apna connection tod liya.

“Tum… unhe control nahi kar sakte,” Raghav ne sthir kadam badhate hue kaha. “Tumhari ambition tumhe andha kar rahi hai. Tum power dekhte ho, lekin jo tumhara nahi, use tum handle nahi kar sakte. Abhi chale jaao, aur kabhi aisa nahi karna, warna aise consequences face karoge jo tum so bhi nahi sakte.”

Aadmi ne use gusse aur dar se dekha. Dheere-dheere, woh Kodipur ki galliyon mein kho gaya, apne muh se threats muttering karte hue. Fragments thodi der tak hover kiye, phir whispers mein badal kar calm acknowledgment mein settle ho gaye.

Dadi ne Raghav ke kandhe par haath rakha. “Tumne betrayal ka saamna kiya — masaazi se nahi, balki present se. Zinda log bhi spirits jitne khatarnak ho sakte hain, agar greed ya dar ne unhe andha kar diya ho. Yaad rakhna, Raghav: tumhari zimmewari sirf yaadon tak nahi, balki zinda aur murda ke beech ke balance tak hai.”

Raghav ne saans chhodi — kaale box ki dhadkan calm ho gayi. Usne samjha ki Kodipur ki history sirf spirits mein likhi nahi, balki un zinda logon ke choices mein bhi hai. Kuch samajh dhoondhte hain, kuch shaanti, aur kuch… power.

Hawa ne halki fufkar carry ki — fragments ki bhi, aur gaon walon ki bhi: “Tum humein guide karte ho… tum humein protect karte ho… tum dono sides ko dekhte ho.”

Raghav ne purane kuein ki taraf dekha — ab chup tha, lekin usko pata tha ki isme secrets aur potential dangers chhupi hain. Kodipur ka balance nazuk hai, aur har action — zinda ya murda — ise ulta kar sakta hai. Usne box ko zor se pakda, vigilant rehne ka faisla kiya.

Aaj raat ne use ek ahem lesson sikhaaya: guardianship sirf masaazi ka saamna karna nahi hai — yeh present ko guide karna, vulnerable ko protect karna, aur unke khilaf khada hona hai jo history ko apne faide ke liye twist karna chahte hain.

Chapter 15: The Weight of a Village

Raati ne Kodipur par ek dabao bhari chuppi chhaayi — pehle kabhi nahi mehsoos hui itni bhaari. Bamboo grove dheemi fufkar raha tha, lekin uske niche kuch andhera mehsoos ho raha tha — na fragment, na vessel… balki kuch jyada vyaapak.

Raghav ne peepal ke ped ki taraf kadam badhaye, kaala box pakde hue, aur seenay par amulet ki dhadkan tez-tez goonj rahi thi. Hawa ghanee ho gayi — raakh, agarbatti, aur kuch khatta-sa — jaise sadion tak dabaa hua pachtaava.

Phir, use dikha. Ek vishaal roop, dhundhla magar solid, chowk ke paas zameen se uth raha tha. Uski shakal barabar badal rahi thi — kabhi insaan jaisi, kabhi haathon-pairon aur chehron ka ek bhayanak gola. Ye Kodipur ki collective memory thi — naslon ka guilt, ek hi bechain aatma mein sameta hua. Uske shareer par jhat se aankhein dikhti thi — har ek mein barabar hissa tha dard ka, gusse ka, aur dar ka.

Raghav tham gaya. Kaala box uske haathon mein dhadak raha tha — warning de raha tha ki ye koi aam encounter nahi hai. Ye spirit na badla maangti thi, na dhyan — bas ek faisla maangti thi.

Ek awaaz figure se goonji, kayi awaazon ka mixture:

“We are the sorrow you inherit… the shame of those who wronged, who lied, who killed, who ignored. You… carry their blood. Will you acknowledge us… or join their denial?”

Dadi Shanta uske paas aa gayi, chehra gambhir. “Ye collective guilt ka embodiment hai, Raghav. Isme gaon ki har injustice, har galat kaam chhupa hai jo kabhi daba diya gaya. Ye rakshak ki himmat test karti hai. Tum ise force se nahi lad sakte. Tumhe iska saamna karna hoga, samajhna hoga, aur isko apne upar uthana hoga.”

Raghav ne gale mein ghutna mehsoos kiya — sadion ka bojh uske oopar dab raha tha. Usne kaala box khola — uski roshni chowk mein veins ki tarah failne lagi. Seenay par amulet dhadak raha tha, spirit ke saath resonate kar raha tha.

“Main tumhe dekhta hoon,” usne kaha, awaaz sthir magar seenay mein darr. “Main tumhara dard dekhta hoon, tumhara gussa, tumhari peeda. Main har injustice ko acknowledge karta hoon, har shikayat ko. Tum yaad rakhaye jaoge, aur tum sunaye jaoge. Kodipur ki history… hamari history… deny nahi ki jaayegi.”

Spirit ne tezi se hilaya — dard ki lashen bahar ki taraf marne lagi, pehle guide kiye hue fragments ko hila diya. Hawa overwhelming energy se bhari hui, lekin fragments ne Raghav ko gher liya — unki maujoodgi stabilizing anchors ban gayi.

“Tum… humein uthaoge,” awaaz ne kaha. “Tum hamare gunah carry karte ho, aur hamari yaad pehchan maangti hai. Agar tum hichkoge, toh poora gaon suffer karega. Kya tum… is bojh ko accept karte ho?”

Raghav ne sawaal ka baraapan mehsoos kiya. Uske poorvajon ka zulm, zinda logon ka dhokha, kayi aatmaon ki bechaini — sab uspar converge ho gaye. Lekin use samajh aa gaya — bachne ka rasta nahi tha. Usne gehri saans li, box ki dhadkan ne use guide kiya, aur usne be-doubt awaaz mein kaha:

“Main accept karta hoon. Main yaadein carry karta hoon, guilt carry karta hoon, dard carry karta hoon — unke neeche doobne ke liye nahi, balki unka samman karne ke liye, unhe guide karne ke liye, aur zinda logon ko masaazi ke qaid mein jaane se bachane ke liye. Main rakshak hoon. Main tumhara bojh uthaunga, aur mere zariye balance wapas laaya jaayega.”

Vishaal spirit ne kamp kiya — jaise uski baaton ki sincerity test kar raha ho. Dheere-dheere, uski kat-ti hui, badalti hui shakal settle hone lagi. Chehre naram hue, haath-pair merge hue, aur uske shareer mein ek shaant chamak failne lagi. Fragments use gher rahe the, dheemi fufkar ke saath:

“We are seen… we are heard… we are remembered…”

Dadi ne uske kandhe par haath rakha. “Tumne sabse bhaari spirit ka saamna kiya hai — gaon ki collective conscience. Kam log aise encounter mein survive kar paaye hain bina khud consume hue. Yaad rakhna, Raghav: guilt aur yaadon ka bojh compassion aur himmat ke saath uthana padta hai.”

Raghav ne saans chhodi — kaala box uske haathon mein halka dhadak raha tha. Use samajh aa gaya — Kodipur ki unrest sirf juratmand spirits ya selfish zinda logon ki wajah se nahi — balki history khud thi, insani galtiyon aur dard ki ek tapestry, jo pehchan maang rahi thi.

Jab vishaal spirit raat mein ghul gaya, ek shaant magar mehsoos hone wali energy chhod kar, Raghav ne apne aap se kaha:

“Main sab kuch uthaunga… dard, guilt, yaadein. Main unhe kabhi nahi bhoolne dunga, aur na hi zinda logon ko unke qaid mein jaane dunga.”

Bamboo grove ne halka sa jhukaya, aur hawa ne fufkarte hue kaha:

“The keeper bears us… the keeper remembers… the keeper protects.”

Pehli baar, Raghav ne apni zimmewari ka asli vistaar mehsoos kiya. Kodipur ka masaal, present, aur future — sab uske haathon mein tha… aur aage ka raasta himmat, aql, aur balance ke liye be-doubt commitment maangta tha.

Chapter 16: The Relic of Binding

Kodipur ki raat chup thi, lekin ek ajeeb si stillness hawa mein thi — jaise gaon khud saans rok ke baitha ho. Raghav gaon ke kinare ki taraf chal raha tha, kaala box haathon mein, seenay par amulet ki dhadkan. Kuch use bulaa raha tha — ek anjaani energy, jo pehle kisi cheez se zyada purani aur taakatwar thi.

Dadi Shanta chupke se peeche chal rahi thi, nazar horizon par. “Ek relic hai,” unhone dheere se kaha. “Jo bhule hue altar se bhi purani hai. Spirits ko bandhne, unrest ko control karne ke liye istemaal hoti thi… lekin kho gayi. Iski taakat bahut hai, lekin khatarnak bhi. Jinhone ise dhoondha, wo khud apni ichhaon mein kha gaye.”

Raghav ka dil dhadka. “Kahan hai?”

Woh use ek jhadiyon se bhari path se guzaar kar ek chhupi hui gufa tak le gayi — uska darwaza belon ki chadar ke peeche chhupa hua tha. Andar, dim light mein nishaan chamak rahe the — wahi nishaan jo usne kaale box par dekhe the. Gufa ke beech ek pathar ka chabootra tha, jispar ek chhoti si intricate cheez — bronze mein chipki hui crystal orb, protection aur command ke nishaanon se taraashi hui.

“The Relic of Binding,” Dadi ne kaha. “Ye tumhari authority ko amplify kar sakti hai spirits par — unhe bhi command kar sakti hai jo abhi tumhare reach se bahar hain… lekin iske consequences hain. Galat istemaal karne par wielder corrupt ho jaata hai, aur control aur tyranny ke beech ki lakeer bahut patli hai.”

Raghav aage badha — orb halka sa dhadak raha tha, jaise zinda ho. Past ke fragments ki chhaayen iske gird ghoom rahi thi, iski energy se kheench kar. Use ek bhayankar kheench mehsoos hui — control ka wada, sabse juratmand spirits ko guide karne ki taakat. Lekin saath hi, ek halki si danger ki fufkar uske dimaag mein goonji:

Use me, and you may lose yourself… lose your purpose… lose your humanity.

Dadi ki awaaz ne uske trance ko toda. “Raghav… tumhe decide karna hoga. Ye relic tumhara bond spirits ke saath majboot kar sakti hai, tumhara kaam aasan kar sakti hai… lekin tumhari morality test karegi. Spirits intent feel karti hain. Agar tumhara dil ek pal bhi hichkega, toh consequences catastrophic ho sakte hain.”

Raghav ne gale mein ghutna mehsoos kiya — faisla ka bojh bhaari tha. Usne orb ki taraf haath badhaya — thandi surface uski ungliyon ke neeche vibrate kar rahi thi. Fragments use gher rahe the, unki fufkar caution aur umeed ka chorus banayi hui:

“We trust you… we follow you… we remember…”

Usne aankhein band ki — kaale box ki energy relic ke saath harmonize hone lagi. Uske saamne tasveeren flash hui — spirits jo apni marzi ke khilaf control hui, gaon jo unrest mein doob gaya, aur uska apna chehra jo power ke corruption se bigad gaya. Phir, Kodipur ki yaad aayi — balance, trust, aur pehchan se zinda, jo uske dimaag mein settle hui.

Usne dheere se haath kheecha. “Main ise nahi use kar sakta,” usne cave mein goonjti hui awaaz mein kaha. “Spirits tools nahi hain. Unka trust aur balance force se nahi aata. Sachchi guidance dar ya power se nahi — samman, samajh, aur pehchan se aati hai. Main khud ko… ya unhe… kho kar nahi risk lunga.”

Relic ki dhadkan kamzor ho gayi — jaise uski choice ka samman kar rahi ho — lekin ek halki si roshni abhi bhi thi. Fragments ne use ek baar phir ghera, dheemi fufkar ke saath:

“You are worthy… you see us… you protect us.”

Dadi ne saans chhodi — unke chehre par relief aur pride dono thi. “Kam log Relic of Binding ke lure ko resist kar paaye. Tumhari restraint tumhari worthiness prove karti hai rakshak ke taur par. Power tempting hoti hai, lekin samajh ke bina control destruction hai.”

Raghav ne relic ko akhri baar dekha, phir gufa se nikal gaya. Use samajh aa gaya — Kodipur ki history powerful objects se bhari hai, lekin sabse khatarnak cheez zinda aur murda ke choices hain. Trust, restraint, aur moral courage hi uske asli tools hain — aur kisi artifact se zyada powerful.

Jab woh raat mein nikle, bamboo grove ne unke gird halka sa sarasaraahat machaya, fragments quiet acknowledgment mein reh gaye:

“We follow your path… we trust your guidance… you bear us without harm.”

Raghav ne kaala box kas ke pakda — zimmewari ka bojh ab uske kandhon par aaram se baith gaya. Kodipur ne use kayi tarah se test kiya tha, lekin har trial ke saath woh bada hua — power se nahi, balki wisdom se. Aur aaj raat, usne prove kiya ki strength domination mein nahi — samajh mein hai.

Hawa ne spirits ki taraf se ek halki, approving murmur carry ki:

“You guide us… you protect us… you are the keeper.”

Chapter 17: Shadows of Ambition

Raghav ne box ko zyada khola — uski roshni ne chowk ko cover kar liya. Usne fragments ki collective memory aur villagers ke trust par focus kiya — jo use protect kar chuke the. Light ne intricate patterns mein flow kiya — intent ko acknowledgment ke saath jodta hua. Aadmi ke summoned shadows bahar ki taraf lashen mari, lekin Raghav ke gird harmonized energy mein penetrate nahi kar paaye.

“Tum control chahte ho,” Raghav ne awaaz uthate hue kaha. “Lekin samajh ke bina power destruction hai. Main is gaon ki yaad carry karta hoon — uska guilt, uska dard, uske sach. Tum ise corrupt nahi kar sakte.”

Fragments aage badhe — Raghav ki energy se jud kar — villagers aur aadmi ke summoned shadows ko gher liya. Chaotic energy dissolve ho gayi — acknowledgment aur guidance ki harmonized dhadkan mein absorb ho gayi. Villagers ne saans li — influence se azad, unki aankhein clear ho gayin.

Aadmi ne gusse mein chillaya jab uski chhaayen dissolve hui — halki si dhuen mein badal kar piche hat gayin. “Ye… ye impossible hai! Tum… tum sabko kaise command kar sakte ho?”

Raghav uske paas aaya — box pakde hue — awaaz shaant magar be-doubt:

“Main unhe command nahi karta. Main unhe guide karta hoon. Trust aur samajh — dar nahi — Kodipur ko ek saath bandhte hain. Tum chale jaoge, aur kabhi aisa nahi karo ge.”

Aadmi ne use lambi nazar se dekha — gusse aur frustration ki jung. Dheere-dheere, woh galliyon mein piche hat gaya — threats muttering karte hue — lekin ab na zinda, na murda — kisi ko bhi manipulate nahi kar sakta tha.

Dadi ne saans chhodi — Raghav ke kandhe par haath rakha. “Tumne zinda ki ambition aur spirits ki unrest — dono ka ek saath saamna kiya hai. Kam log itne pressure mein balance maintain kar paaye hain. Yaad rakhna, Raghav: ek rakshak ka asli test control nahi — guidance hai.”

Raghav ne kaale box ko mehsoos kiya — ab comfortably settle ho gaya tha. Fragments use gher rahe the, dheemi fufkar ke saath:

“We trust you… we follow you… you bear us with care.”

Jab raat ki hawa ne bamboo grove mein sarasaraahat machayi, use apni zimmewari ka scale samajh aa gaya. Kodipur sirf masaazi ka gaon nahi tha — yeh ek zinda tapestry tha, nazuk magar bardasht karne wala — uski judgment aur restraint par nirbhar. Aur use pata tha — har challenge ab sirf himmat nahi, balki wisdom, empathy, aur ek be-doubt moral compass maangega.

Pehli baar, Raghav ko ek gehri yaqeen hui: woh taiyyar hai — chahe Kodipur ka masaal, present, ya future kuch bhi fek de.

Chapter 18: The Lovers’ Wail

Kodipur unusually chup tha — chandni ne bamboo grove par silver glow chhaaya hua tha. Raghav ke haathon mein kaala box halka dhadak raha tha — jaise paas mein unrest ki warning de raha ho. Lekin ye presence anger ya ambition se paida nahi hui thi — ye dard tha, gehra aur amar.

Purane kuein ke paas, halki cheekhein goonj rahi thi — hawa ke saath upar neeche hoti hui. Wo mournful thi, magar urgent — despair se bhari hui jo Raghav ke rongton tak khade kar deti thi. Usne awaaz ko follow kiya — abandoned haveli ke ruins ke paas ek group villagers ka ikattha hua — unke chehre dar mein jamaye hue.

Do figures — chandni mein mushkil se dikhai deti — courtyard ke upar ghoom rahi thi. Unki shaklein delicate, almost ethereal — ek dard bhari jhappi mein lipat kar. Ek young woman — chehra pale, aankhein aansuon se bhigi hui. Dusra — ek aadmi, aankhon mein tanhaai. Unki fufkar hawa mein fail rahi thi — ek hi naam bar-bar repeat karti hui:

“Ragini… Dev…”

Raghav turant samajh gaya — ye ordinary unrest nahi tha. Yeh lovers sadion pehle tragically mare the — unka bandhan violent circumstances ne toda tha. Unki aatmaen reh gayin — chhodne se inkar kar rahi thi — aur ab unka dard zinda logon ko apne qaid mein kheenchne ki koshish kar raha tha — jinke naam similar the ya jo unke families se the.

Dadi uske paas aa gayi, chehra gambhir. “Ye attachment aur longing se feed karti hain, Raghav. Agar hum intervene nahi karenge, toh woh aur souls ko apne grief mein kheench lenge — zinda logon ko apne despair se bandh denge.”

Raghav ne sar hilaaya — zimmewari ka bojh uspar settle ho gaya. “Main unhe guide karunga… gently. Unhe acknowledgment chahiye, force nahi.”

Woh aage badha — kaala box khola. Light softly faili — harsh nahi, commanding nahi — courtyard mein weave hoti hui, spirits ko chhu gayi. “Main tumhara dard dekhta hoon,” usne dheemi awaaz mein kaha. “Main tumhari peeda jaanta hoon. Tumne gehri mohabbat ki thi, aur tumhe zulm hua. Tumhari kahani yaad rakhayi jaayegi — lekin zinda log tumhare dard ke liye suffer nahi kar sakte. Tumhe shaanti leni hogi.”

Lovers ne tadap kar hilaya — unki shaklein flicker hui — acknowledgment ka virodh kar rahi thi. Dard ki lashen nearby villagers ki taraf badhi — unhe spectral embrace mein kheenchne ki koshish. Raghav ne focus kiya — pehle guide kiye hue fragments ki energy ko box ki roshni ke saath mix kiya.

“Tum yaad rakhaye jaoge,” usne softly continue kiya. “Tumhari mohabbat sach thi, tumhara dard ma’loom tha. Lekin jo zinda duniya mein chalte hain — unhe free hona chahiye. Unhe chhod do. Khud ko araam do.”

Fufkar ruki, cheekhein komal hui, aur spirits ki shaklein halki roshni mein dissolve hone lagi. Aakhri baar unki aankhon se longing guzri — phir woh poori tarah vanish ho gaye — apne peeche ek shaant garmi chhod kar.

Villagers ne palke jhapkayin — lovers ki unrest ke emotional grip se azad. Kuch logon ne naam dheemi awaaz mein repeat kiye — kahani yaad kar rahe the, bina uske dard ko feel kiye.

Dadi ne Raghav ke kandhe par haath rakha. “Tumne unhe freed kiya — power se nahi, samajh se. Murda log zinda se tab tak chipke rehte hain jab tak unhe dekha nahi jaata. Tumne unhe woh pehchan di, aur woh release ho gaye.”

Raghav ne saans chhodi — kaale box ki dhadkan calm rhythm mein aa gayi. Use apne kaam ki subtlety samajh aa gayi — sirf gusse ya ambition ka saamna karna kaafi nahi tha. Dard, mohabbat, gham — sab empathy, careful guidance, aur unki stories ka samman maangte the.

Jab raat ki hawa ne bamboo grove mein sarasaraahat machayi, fragments ki taraf se gratitude ki halki fufkar goonji:

“You see us… you honor us… you carry our stories.”

Raghav ne haveli ke ruins ki taraf dekha — samajh aa gaya ki Kodipur ki history sirf gusse aur dhokhe mein zinda nahi — mohabbat aur loss mein bhi hai. Gaon ki tapestry intricate, nazuk, aur khoobsurat thi — lekin ek aise rakshak ki zaroorat thi jo dard aur gusse dono ko barabar care se navigate kar sake.

Usne box ko zor se pakda — jo bhi aage wait kar raha ho, uske liye ready — jaante hue ki aage ka raasta sirf himmat nahi, dil bhi maangega.

Chapter 19: The Festival of the Unseen

Kodipur crescent moon ke neeche bechaini se so raha tha. Hawa ki fufkar aaj zyada sharp thi — urgency aur unease se bhari hui. Kaala box Raghav ke seenay par halka dhadak raha tha — warning de raha tha ki kuch bada uth raha hai — sirf ek spirit se zyada.

Dadi Shanta use chowk par milin, chehra gambhir. “Raghav,” unhone quietly kaha, “Kodipur ki history mein ek curse chhupi hui hai. Sadion pehle, villagers ek festival celebrate karte the — zinda aur murda dono ka samman karne ke liye — ‘The Festival of the Unseen’. Lekin greed, betrayal, aur neglect ne ise kharab kar diya. Curse dormant padi rahi — balance disturb hone ka intezaar kar rahi thi.”

Raghav ki aankhein tight hui. “Aur aaj raat?”

“Haan. Conditions perfect hain. Fragments restless hain, villagers unaware hain, aur hawa masaazi ki yaad carry kar rahi hai. Agar ye fully awaken ho gayi, toh kayi spirits ek saath uth sakti hain — be-kaboo — zinda aur murda dono par havoc macha sakti hain.”

Usne kaale box ka bojh mehsoos kiya — dhadkan tez ho gayi. “Hum ise rokne… guide karne… restrain karne ki koshish karenge — chaos banne se pehle.”

Woh abandoned festival grounds ki taraf gaye — ek wide courtyard, jisme purane pathar ke columns the. Festival ke nishaan — ab fade — zameen par taraashe hue the. Pehle guide kiye hue fragments paas hi the — energy surge ko mehsoos kar rahe the. Hawa anticipation se bhari hui — sadion ka latent gussa, dard, confusion.

Raghav ne ghutne tek kar nishaanon ko trace kiya — pathar ke neeche ki energy ko feel kiya. “Ye curse memory se judi hai,” usne realize kiya. “Punishment nahi… neglect. Festival murda ka samman karne, unki stories acknowledge karne ke liye tha. Jab ise chhod diya gaya, toh spirits ki energy beemar ho gayi.”

Achanak, ek thandi hawa ne courtyard ko cross kiya. Har kone se chhaayen nikli — history ke bhule hue log — har ek gusse, dar, aur tanhaai se manifest hua. Kuch fragments jo use pata the, kuch bilkul naye — curse ki pull se kheench kar aaye.

Kaala box chamak utha jab Raghav khada hua — use amulet ke saath harmonize karne ke liye uthaya. “Main rakshak hoon. Main tumhe dekhta hoon. Main tumhe yaad karta hoon. Tum acknowledged ho, aur tumhara dard ignore nahi kiya jaayega. Lekin tum zinda logon ko nuksaan nahi pahunchaoge.”

Spirits ne use test kiya — energy chaotic, overwhelm karne wali. Raghav ne focus kiya — pehle guide kiye hue fragments use gherne lage — unki collective energy stabilizers ban gayi. Hawa mein fufkar bhari hui:

“We follow… we trust… we remember…”

Usne festival ke patterns ko carefully trace kiya — box ki roshni ko nishaanon ke saath harmonize karne diya. Dheere-dheere, spirits ka gussa kam hua — recognition ne replace kiya. Unki shaklein naram hui, chehre relax hue — sadion ka neglect acknowledge ho gaya.

Dadi ne kaha, “Tum festival ki yaadon ko reality mein wapas weave kar rahe ho. Har spirit jo tum guide karte ho, balance restore karta hai — chaos ko zinda aur murda dono ko consume hone se bachata hai.”

Raghav ne apna kaam carefully continue kiya — energy ko evenly flow karne diya — zinda villagers aur spirits ke beech. Jab subah ki pehli kirne ne bamboo grove ko chhua — curse neutralize ho chuki thi — destroy nahi, balki harmonize. Festival ki yaad — jo ek waqt tainted thi — ab acknowledgment aur balance se restore ho chuki thi.

Fragments ne use akhri baar ghera — fufkar faint magar clear:

“You have seen us… you honor us… you restore us.”

Raghav ne saans chhodi — kaala box morning wind ke saath gentle rhythm mein dhadak raha tha. Use samajh aa gaya — Kodipur ka asli khatra spirits khud nahi — yaadon ki neglect thi — masaazi ka samman nahi karna, unhe acknowledge nahi karna, unse seekhna nahi.

Jab villagers ne neend se jaaga, itne close chaos se bachne ka ehsaas bhi nahi hua — Raghav ne zimmewari ka bojh pehle se zyada tez mehsoos kiya. Zinda aur murda, masaal aur present — sab balance uspar nirbhar tha.

Aur pehli baar, use samajh aa gaya: rakshak hona sirf spirits ko guide karna nahi — poore gaon ki nazuk harmony preserve karna tha — ek ek yaad ko sambhal kar.

Chapter 20: The Keeper’s Last Stand

Kodipur ki raat pehle kabhi nahi dekhi gayi thi. Bamboo grove urgently fufkar raha tha — past ki unrest ki fragments carry kar raha tha. Kaala box Raghav ke seenay par dhadak raha tha — roshni steady magar intense — warning de raha tha ki gaon ka final trial aa chuka hai.

Chowk se chhaayen unnaturally move kar rahi thi. Har threat ka culmination — forgotten king ka gussa, living betrayer ki ambition, sorrowful lovers, aur festival curse ki restless memories. Har fragment, har spirit — ek saath chaotic energy mein surge kar rahi thi — Raghav ki control ki limits test kar rahi thi.

Dadi Shanta uske paas aa gayi — awaaz shaant magar firm. “Ye wahi waqt hai, Raghav. Har choice jo tumne ki, har life jo tumne protect ki, har fragment jo tumne guide kiya — sab isi point ki taraf le kar aaye. Aaj raat, tum unhe unify karoge, warna Kodipur ruin mein gir jaayega.”

Raghav ne gehri saans li — kaala box uthaya. Uski roshni ne gaon ko cover kar liya — seenay par amulet ke saath resonate hui. Fragments turant respond kiye — perfect synchrony mein use gher liya. Hawa mein fufkar bhari hui:

“We follow… we trust… we remember…”

Usne aankhein band ki — command karne par nahi, acknowledge karne par focus kiya. Har spirit aur fragment se bola — unhe bataya ki unki stories dekhi gayi hain, unka dard recognize hua hai, unki peeda ka samman hua hai.

“You are remembered. You are acknowledged. You are not forgotten.”

Hawa ne kamp kiya jab forgotten king ki chhaayen aage badhi — lekin Raghav ne acknowledgment ki yaadon ko use wash karne diya. Lovers ki cheekhein goonji — lekin usne space mein unki mohabbat, unka tragedy, aur unka release infuse kar diya. Festival curse ki goonj balance se shimmer karne lagi — uski energy se gently guide hui.

Andhere se, living betrayer ne akhri baar attack kiya — “Tum sabko control nahi kar sakte!” chillaya, shadows bahar ki taraf lashen mari.

Raghav ki aankhein khuli — shaant aur resolute. “Main control nahi karta. Main guide karta hoon. Main samman karta hoon. Tum zinda logon ko nuksaan nahi pahunchaoge, aur spirits ko gulam nahi banaoge.”

Kaale box ki ek sweep mein, light ki pulse bahar faili — har fragment, har spirit ko harmonize kar diya — betrayer ki chaotic chhaayen neutralize kar di. Nearby villagers ne palke jhapkayin — influence se azad — fragments perfect order mein align ho gaye — unki fufkar ek unified song ban gayi:

“You are worthy… you see us… you protect us.”

Betrayer lurch karta hua piche hat gaya — haar gaya force se nahi — har memory ke combined acknowledgment se jo usne manipulate karne ki koshish ki thi. Woh chhaayon mein vanish ho gaya — uski ambition extinguish — past aur present ki unity ke aage powerless.

Raghav ne saans chhodi — energy settle ho gayi. Har fragment, har spirit — ab shaant hover kar rahe the — unki unrest resolve ho chuki thi. Kodipur ka chowk — jo tension aur dar se bhaari tha — ab gratitude ki halki fufkar se bhara hua tha.

Dadi ne uske kandhe par haath rakha — aankhein chamak rahi thi. “Tumne kar dikhaya, Raghav. Tumne ek rakshak ke sabse bade test ka saamna kiya — masaazi ka gussa, present ka dhokha, aur collective dard — sab ek saath. Tumne balance preserve kiya. Kodipur safe hai.”

Raghav ne gaon, glowing fragments, aur bamboo grove mein sarasaraati hawa ki taraf dekha. Use apni journey ka full scope samajh aa gaya — ye kabhi spirits ko defeat karna ya power wield karna nahi tha. Ye recognition tha, empathy thi, aur be-doubt moral courage.

Fragments ne akhri baar ghera — ek saath fufkar ke saath:

“You are the keeper… you carry us… you honor us… you protect us.”

Gaon par ek garam shaanti chhaayi. Kaale box ki roshni soft ho gayi, amulet ki dhadkan slow, aur raat ki hawa halki — jaise shaant harmony se zinda ho.

Raghav ne finally muskuraya — rakshak hona ka asli matlab samajh kar. Kodipur ki history — uska gussa, dard, mohabbat, aur guilt — sab acknowledge ho chuka tha. Zinda aur murda balance mein coexist kar sakte the — aur woh unke beech ka bridge ban chuka tha.

Jab subah ki pehli kirne ne Kodipur ko chhua — suraj ki roshni ne bamboo ke patton par halka prakash bikhera. Fragments ki fufkar peaceful hum mein badal gayi — gratitude aur acknowledgment carry karti hui.

Raghav ne kaala box apne paas rakha — apne aap se kaha:

“I will always see you… I will always carry you… I will always protect you.”
Kodipur ab unrest se haunted gaon nahi tha. Yeh ek aisa sthal tha jahan memory aur life ek saath rehte the — aur Raghav, the keeper — ne akhirkaar apna purpose poora kar liya tha.
